mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and margin calls that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and industrial production indicates a structural demand-supply mismatch. When housing activity decelerates while manufacturing output remains elevated, inventory accumulation pressures compress corporate margins and drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a rolling 3-year regression, or SPX forward returns do not decline when HOUST falls and INDPRO rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hikes, rising consumer prices erode real disposable income and increase input costs. This inflationary pressure combined with tightening liquidity forces a repricing of growth assets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both the federal funds rate and CPI index rise concurrently for three consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real consumer credit outstanding reflects expanding household purchasing power and economic momentum. This direct demand signal supports corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS growth rates remains below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between credit expansion and contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial production below a critical threshold signals supply chain disruptions or capacity constraints that trigger immediate profit warnings. This supply shock forces downward revisions to earnings estimates, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO drops below the 110 threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate determines labor market tightness. When payroll additions exceed 200,000 monthly while unemployment remains below 5.0%, wage-push inflation expectations rise, prompting discount rate repricing and negative equity returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both strong payroll growth and low unemployment coincide, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by peak housing activity and slowing GDP growth, real estate investment reversals drag down financial sector earnings. This sector-specific deterioration transmits to the broader index, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when housing starts peak and GDP growth decelerates simultaneously, or the signal fails to predict financial sector underperformance.
